And so, I first wanted to provide just a little bit of background, about why we built Revenue Management in the first place, and our philosophy, and then I'll show you around the product.
I think our goal really was, to take what has been proven to work about revenue management, and then… try to modernize the approach.
And what we'd heard from, over the last 18 months, we've been working with, about a dozen of our customers, all the way from 200,000 unit portfolios to 5,000 unit portfolios.

And about 18 months ago, we had a number of our customers that started to approach us and say, we would… we would really like what you did for market surveys, but can you do it for revenue management as well?
And here are the pain points that we have currently.
There were a lot of folks that were moving away from RealPage for any number of reasons, the most common of which is the same reason that you all did.
And so the first priority was always, okay, how do we integrate public data into a revenue management system?
And so RealPage and Yieldstar always, talked about how their system had, you know, 6 million leases that they were able to train the model on.

And because of our Apartment IQ data and the scale that that's at, I'm gonna talk a little bit about our system, but we've actually built this system out of the more than 16 million leases we've observed that are all publicly available.

So, our system, was really meant to do, one, one of the core pain points that we had heard, or areas of potential improvement for Yieldstar, was providing more context as to why the system is making the recommendation it's making.
Some folks that are very technical and new to the system, they could understand it and get it very quickly, but often your site-level folks, or even people internally.
We're wondering, dang, it just made this big price increase or price decrease recommendation after not moving for 60 days before that.
Why?

And so, our first priority was, how do we make sure that you have The equivalent level of transparency into a pricing model recommendation that you would have if you had an analyst, one of our analysts sitting right next to you at your desk.
So that was our first challenge that we wanted to take on, and we'll talk a little bit about that.
The second was, how do we bring market data into a revenue management system in a compliant way?
And that's our ApartmentIQ data.
So, we've got almost 5 years now, 5 years in October, which is the largest database of publicly available comp data by far in the industry.
And we brought that and we integrated it into our revenue management tool.
Both visually, you'll be able to see all that comp data integrated directly, but it's also integrated To the recommendations the system makes.
So we'll talk a little bit about that.
We also had heard that sometimes the interfaces could get a bit complex.
We'll talk about this a bit on renewals.
So there was an opportunity to try to simplify, especially, again, for site-level and regional-level folks that are in the product using it every day.

And then finally, these systems did end up getting very expensive, and because of advances, in artificial intelligence, because of the size of our ApartmentIQ database, we were able to build a full, fully built-out system that we think is very modern, has a number of improvements over the legacy systems, with a substantially less of an investment to build that, and we want to pass those costs on to the industry as well.
So, those were… that was our target.
It's what we heard from our customers, and that's why we built revenue management.
Couple more slides just to give you a sense of what's under the hood here, before I actually show it to you.

I mentioned that we have about 16 million leases that we've observed, publicly, which means we've just seen the unit come on the website, and then come off the website and stay off the website, which means it wasn't a cancel or a denial.
And what that enables us to do is create a system that's very predictive.
All of those data points we combine with a new form of artificial intelligence called explainable AI.

So, was it because your recent leasing velocity is slow?
Was it because your asking rents are out of whack with your recently achieved rents?

phase of this.

So, for each of these pricing recommendations, what we've heard from folks is often you get a recommendation, and the number one question is why, and then you have to end up sifting through and playing detective in an existing system to try and understand that.
Instead of that, we want to just show you exactly what the features are in the model that are driving that recommendation.
And so, there's about 9 features in our model that are the primary features that are making the recommendations and projecting how many leases you're going to get at what price point.
You're seeing a few of them here.

The highest and most important one here is just they're just not getting enough recent leasing velocity to justify the amount of leases they need to sign over the coming 84-day period, and so you can see in the upper right-hand corner, this is a high negative impact, on the recommendations right now.
Couple other highlights that I just want to point out.
For folks that are used to using Yieldstar, you're accustomed to this idea of, how do my asking rents compare to my lease rents?
You know, what am I asking versus what have I achieved?
And our model is always going to look at this on a net effective rent basis.
We're going to do it on a net effective rent per square foot to correct for any floor plan differences.
So, we're always going to look at how is what you're asking compared to your average last 5 leases?
And then we're actually… the model's also going to be looking at how does what you're asking on a market position basis, how does that compare to where you've typically leased as well?
So, a lot of properties are able to find a nice little niche.
Where they're able to be pretty impactful, and move a lot of leases.
And so, we're always gauging not only just how many leases has your property signed now, but where do you tend to lease on a market basis?
And so, the model's gonna be looking at that as well, and that's, of course, something unique we can do because of our apartment IQ data.
So this is part of the explainability.
